vertical.description: Vertical extent defined by geometric height levels in meters above the WGS84 ellipsoid, ranging from 5m to 6000m. These levels are commonly used for atmospheric and environmental datasets.

pressureLevels.vrs: PARAMETRICCRS["WMO standard atmosphere layer 0",PDATUM["Mean Sea Level",ANCHOR["101325 Pa at 15°C"]],CS[parametric,1],AXIS["pressure (hPa)",down],PARAMETRICUNIT["hPa",1.0]]
pressureLevels.description: Vertical extent defined by standard atmospheric pressure levels in hectopascals (hPa), ranging from 1000 hPa (near surface) to 10 hPa (upper stratosphere). The vertical coordinate reference system follows the WMO standard atmosphere, with pressure increasing downward. These levels are commonly used in meteorological and climate datasets for representing atmospheric layers.

temporal.description: Forecast are produced four times daily:longer runs at T00Z and T12Z, and shorter runs at T06Z and T18Z. Each run covers 7 days (168 hours), with 1-hourly timesteps up to 54 hours, 3-hourly timesteps from 57 to 144 hours, and 6-hourly timesteps thereafter. The shortened runs provide forecasts up to 67 hours.

properties.title: Met Office Global Deterministic
properties.description: The Met Office's flagship Numerical Weather Prediction system, the Unified Model, underpins both weather and climate prediction and is used in several operational configurations. The Global deterministic 10km model is a global configuration of the Unified Model, delivering highly accurate short-range deterministic forecasts for up to six or seven days, and is recognised as one of the most advanced national meteorological services. Forecasts are generated four times daily: extended runs at 00:00 and 12:00 UTC, and shorter runs at 06:00 and 18:00 UTC.

concepts.title: dataType
concepts.description: Predictions and Post Processed data are defined as data that infers a full coverage of the state of the physical, chemical and/or biological environment within a given domain and specific context. This includes data assimilation analysis, projections and predictions to different times and post processed outputs derived from predictions. Data are produced using automated production typically in large volumes controlled by algorithms and data science. Prediction data can take many forms, but they always have a Geospatial Feature, Coverage or native grids on creation. They may be packaged differently for supply for customer requirement.

diagnostic.title: Diagnostic
diagnostic.description: The diagnostic filter parameter is the title property of the concept object within the themes array, where the scheme is 'https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/standard_name/'.
